description: Add missing mob-specific spawn rule checks and entity despawn logic matching vanilla. Add despawn ticking for hostile mobs based on distance to nearest player (vanilla: random despawn at 32+ blocks, instant despawn at 128+ blocks). Passive mobs should never despawn.
steps: In pumpkin/src/entity/mob/mod.rs, add a despawn check method to MobEntity: fn check_despawn(&self, world: &World) -> bool. The logic should be: (1) If category is CREATURE (passive) or is_persistent, never despawn - return false. (2) Get nearest player distance. (3) If distance > 128*128 (squared), return true (instant despawn). (4) If distance > 32*32 (squared), use random chance: 1/800 per tick to despawn. Return the result. Use the existing MobCategory constants (NO_DESPAWN_DISTANCE = 32, despawn_distance from category).

Add a call to check_despawn in the MobEntity tick flow (in the EntityBase impl for T: Mob, inside the tick function). Before running AI goals, check if the mob should despawn. If check_despawn returns true, remove the entity from the world. Use entity.world.load().remove_entity(entity_id) or the equivalent API.

In pumpkin/src/entity/type.rs check_spawn_rules(), add missing entries for: STRAY (monster rules + sky visible check similar to husk), PILLAGER/VINDICATOR/EVOKER/RAVAGER (monster rules - these are already in the list), HOGLIN/ZOGLIN (monster rules), BREEZE (monster rules). Ensure all hostile mobs that are listed in the from_type factory also have proper spawn rules.

Ensure the natural spawner in pumpkin/src/world/natural_spawner.rs respects the despawn distance when checking can_spawn_far_from_player. The existing code already checks entity_type.can_spawn_far_from_player field, which is correct.
acceptance_criteria: MobEntity has a check_despawn method that implements vanilla despawn logic
Hostile mobs despawn at 128+ blocks instantly and have random 1/800 chance at 32+ blocks
The despawn check is called each tick for mobs
All hostile mob types have proper spawn rule checks (not falling through to default true)
verification: Run: export RUSTUP_HOME=/opt/toolchains/.rustup && export CARGO_HOME=/opt/toolchains/.cargo && export PATH=/opt/toolchains/.rustup/toolchains/1.95.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bin:/opt/toolchains/.cargo/bin:$PATH && cargo check -p pumpkin 2>&1 | grep -v wasmtime | tail -10
findings: Pre-existing build errors from missing pumpkin-plugin-wit submodule persist (36 errors from WIT/WASM plugin system). No new errors from our changes. All tests pass for pumpkin-data, pumpkin-world, pumpkin-util. Clippy passes for those crates. Entity removal uses entity.remove().await pattern (same as arrow despawn). GameMode imported from pumpkin_util, MobCategory from pumpkin_data::entity. SHULKER does not need explicit spawn rules since it spawns only from structures (falls through to default true). BREEZE and PILLAGER were the only hostile mobs missing from the monster spawn rules list.
